_meta.source: Public podcast pages, re-verified line by line on 2026-06-12 (URLs curl-tested, hosts checked against show pages)
_meta.warning: Audience numbers are public estimates and shift quickly. Re-check the show's media kit before a final pitch. Where no public guest-submission route exists, the contact_hint says so instead of inventing one.

no_priors.name: No Priors
no_priors.focus: Frontier AI, agent infrastructure, foundation-model startups
no_priors.audience_size: Major tech podcast (Spotify top tech charts); audience estimates are unverified
no_priors.host_angle: Sarah pushes deep technical implementation; Elad pushes economics + go-to-market. Both interested in agent orchestration architecture, not generic LLM chat.
no_priors.recent_episode_url: https://www.sierra.ai/resources/podcasts (NOTE: Bret Taylor episode, Ep. 82, Sept 2024 — hosted on Sierra's site; used as a positioning reference, not a recent episode)
no_priors.contact_hint: Sarah Guo on X @saranormous; Elad Gil at eladgil.com — pitch via X DM preferred
no_priors.recommended_typewise_angle: Multi-agent orchestration that ships INSIDE existing helpdesks instead of replacing them — the 'augment vs replace' thesis Sierra+Decagon don't credibly play. Technology co-developed with ETH Zurich researchers gives a deep-tech anchor.

the_cx_cast.name: The CX Cast
the_cx_cast.focus: Customer experience strategy for Fortune 500 buyers
the_cx_cast.audience_size: Forrester analyst audience; B2B enterprise CS leaders
the_cx_cast.host_angle: Forrester is data-led: bring research, comparative metrics, named customer outcomes. They distrust pitches without numbers.
the_cx_cast.contact_hint: Forrester-run show — guests are typically Forrester analysts or clients; lowest-friction route is LinkedIn to a current host (lineup rotates)
the_cx_cast.recommended_typewise_angle: Brack.ch story: multilingual Swiss retail, AI agent assist with measured efficiency gains. EU/Swiss hosting available as an enterprise differentiator vs US-default competitors.

modern_customer_podcast.name: The Modern Customer Podcast
modern_customer_podcast.focus: Customer experience trends, AI in CX, future-of-work
modern_customer_podcast.host_angle: Blake favours examples over abstractions. She wants a customer story with a number attached.
modern_customer_podcast.contact_hint: LinkedIn DM. Her site is blakemichellemorgan.com — careful: blakemorgan.com is NOT her site.
modern_customer_podcast.recommended_typewise_angle: How an AI layer with 200+ claimed integrations ships inside the helpdesk you already run, in days — not the 6-month migration timeline of replacement platforms. Lead with Brack.ch (~30% efficiency story at DPD Switzerland as backup).

be_customer_led.name: Be Customer Led
be_customer_led.focus: Operating CX at scale; CX-as-a-discipline inside B2B
be_customer_led.audience_size: Niche but high signal — CS/CX directors and VPs as listener base
be_customer_led.host_angle: Bill cares about CX ops and tooling decisions. The 'augment, don't replace' framing fits his audience perfectly.
be_customer_led.recommended_typewise_angle: Why CS directors should add AI as a helpdesk layer (not a rip-and-replace), with EU/Swiss hosting as the board-defensible compliance angle.

punk_cx.name: Punk CX
punk_cx.focus: Contrarian CX takes, AI augmentation, anti-incumbent angles
punk_cx.host_angle: Adrian likes provocations. Lead with what's wrong with the current AI-in-CS narrative, then position Typewise as the fix.
punk_cx.recommended_typewise_angle: Contrarian: most AI CS vendors are selling chatbot replacement; Typewise is the layer that respects the helpdesk you already paid for, with EU/Swiss hosting available.

support_driven_podcast.name: Support Driven Podcast
support_driven_podcast.host: Community-hosted (Inside Support Driven is hosted by community founder Scott Tran — verify current podcast lineup before pitching)
support_driven_podcast.focus: Customer support leadership, team operations, tool decisions
support_driven_podcast.audience_size: Connected to the Support Driven community (site claims 30,000+ CS professionals) — direct buyer reach
support_driven_podcast.host_angle: Practitioners. They want to hear what tooling actually moved an agent's day, not what the marketing deck says.
support_driven_podcast.contact_hint: Via the Support Driven community (supportdriven.com) — confirm the current podcast host before any direct pitch
support_driven_podcast.recommended_typewise_angle: Frontline-agent perspective: how an agent-assist layer inside the existing helpdesk changes the volume per agent per day. Bring an agent (not a marketer) on the episode.

twenty_vc.name: 20VC
twenty_vc.audience_size: One of the highest-reach VC podcasts; Fortune 500 / VC LP listener base
twenty_vc.host_angle: Harry likes founder-origin stories with a clean inflection. The B2C-keyboard-to-B2B-agent pivot is exactly the narrative arc that works here.
twenty_vc.contact_hint: Harry Stebbings on X @HarryStebbings, accessible by DM if the pitch is sharp
twenty_vc.recommended_typewise_angle: Pivot story: B2C Typewise keyboard → B2B agent platform → 50+ enterprise customers including Unilever / DPD Switzerland. ETH Zurich research roots. EU deep-tech.

saastr_podcast.name: SaaStr Podcast
saastr_podcast.focus: B2B SaaS scaling, founder-led GTM, ARR milestones
saastr_podcast.audience_size: SaaStr community is the canonical B2B SaaS audience
saastr_podcast.host_angle: Jason is a numbers guy. Come with ARR trajectory, customer count, NRR, or don't come. Pivot stories also welcomed.
saastr_podcast.contact_hint: Guest submission form: saastr.com/podcast-guest-submission/ ; or LinkedIn DM to Jason Lemkin
saastr_podcast.recommended_typewise_angle: EU-anchored B2B SaaS that wins enterprise without forcing a platform migration. Public customer logos: Unilever, DPD Switzerland, IVECO, Beurer, Galaxus, Brack.ch.

lennys_podcast.name: Lenny's Podcast
lennys_podcast.focus: Product, growth, B2B SaaS — operator interviews
lennys_podcast.audience_size: Top-tier product-management audience; 1,000,000+ newsletter subscribers (per lennysnewsletter.com About page)
lennys_podcast.host_angle: Lenny wants operator playbooks, not vision. The Typewise growth angle should be 'how a ~20-person company is competing with Sierra/Decagon on a non-paid channel strategy.'
lennys_podcast.contact_hint: Lenny Rachitsky on LinkedIn or X — no public guest-submission form is documented; cold outreach with a sharp operator story
lennys_podcast.recommended_typewise_angle: Growth playbook: how non-paid channels (MCP server for devs, founder LinkedIn, DACH community) win against well-funded US competitors. Show the radar.

acquired.name: Acquired
acquired.focus: Long-form company histories, strategy, tech ecosystems
acquired.audience_size: Top-tier business podcast; Fortune 500 + VC + tech-founder reach
acquired.host_angle: They cover scaled companies, not pre-Series-B startups. Pitch only when Typewise has a more public narrative arc (e.g. Series B announced). Useful to watch as a future channel, not today.
acquired.contact_hint: hello@acquired.fm (published site contact) — but unlikely to land at Typewise's current stage; track for later
acquired.recommended_typewise_angle: Reserved for after a major funding or growth milestone. Today: track and plant the narrative seed (B2C-to-B2B pivot, ETH research, EU enterprise differentiator).
